title: austroblechnum lehmannii
intro: Austroblechnum lehmannii, also known as the giant chain fern or giant wood fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Austroblechnum lehmannii is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be applied topically in the form of creams or ointments.
